title: Under the Sea Wind
excerpt: Carson's first book - a vivid, novelistic portrait of Atlantic marine life told through the journeys of individual sea creatures.
keyIdeas: Marine life can be understood through the individual stories of its creatures
The Atlantic coast is a corridor of migration, feeding, and reproduction for countless species
Birds, fish, and marine mammals are linked in a complex web of predation and interdependence
The sea's rhythms govern the lives of all shoreline creatures
keyTakeaways: Each creature from sand flea to osprey plays a vital role in the marine ecosystem
The cycles of the sea shape the lives of all shore creatures
Human activities along the coast ripple through the entire food web
The drama of survival in the sea is as compelling as any human story
whoShouldRead: Anyone who loves Carson's prose style
whoShouldSkip: Readers seeking a structured textbook or field guide
Those who prefer straight science to narrative
